meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Stop Abandoning Projects: The Student's Client Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Struggling to finish your first app? Use this expert client workflow guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your portfolio projects faster.
content.answer_block: The secret to finishing projects isn't more code; it's a rigid client workflow that forces shipping. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative execution, you eliminate decision fatigue. Break your build into micro-milestones, automate the boilerplate, and treat your personal projects like high-stakes commercial client contracts.
content.intro:
The graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories is paved with good intentions. You start a weekend project with fire in your eyes, only to hit the 'middle-of-the-app' wall where complexity explodes and motivation vanishes. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a production-grade workflow.

This guide replaces your chaotic coding style with a professional client workflow. We are going to treat your portfolio project like a high-paying contract, utilizing Antigravity to structure your intent and Claude Code to crush the execution. It is time to stop dreaming and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Stop building features that don't matter. Define the absolute minimum functionality required to solve the user's problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a 3-feature MVP spec for a [Project Name]. Focus on maximum impact with minimum code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap with 3 core milestones for this MVP'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Before writing code, map your data. A clear schema prevents the 'refactoring hell' that kills most student projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema and state management plan for a [Project Name] using a modular architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the schema files based on the project requirements'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate
steps.description: Don't waste hours on setup. Use modern tooling to scaffold your environment so you can jump straight into business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a [Tech Stack] app including linting, testing, and CI/CD configuration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project structure using the generated boilerplate'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build in 2-hour sprints. Never move to the next feature until the current one is tested and passing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the first milestone into 4-6 granular coding tasks. Provide a checklist for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the authentication module as defined in task 1'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write tests for your features. If it isn't tested, it doesn't exist in your production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the [Feature Name] module to ensure edge-case reliability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write and execute tests for the current implementation'

pro_tips: Always commit your code after every successfully passing test; it builds psychological momentum.
If you feel stuck on a feature for more than 60 minutes, delete the branch and restart with a simpler approach.
Treat your README.md as a living document; update it daily to track your progress and keep your 'Why' in focus.
Use a 'Done is better than perfect' mantra—ship a functioning MVP before you ever touch CSS polish.
Set a hard deadline for your project; treat it like a client deliverable with a penalty for late submission.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel or Netlify, share the link in our community discord, and start planning your next iteration based on real user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by senior engineers to ship professional software. Stop letting your ideas die in a private repo and start building a portfolio that actually gets you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
